event_blocks.summary: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang, clad in his characteristic black leather jacket, black shirt, and glasses, stood on a dark stage, highlighting 'MARCH 18, 2024' and a purple 'y/ highlights' logo, to discuss the 'Isaac reinforcement learning gym' that enables a 'humanoid robot' to learn adaptation to the physical world. He then introduced a powerful 'incredible computer' designed to drive both robotic cars and a 'humanoid robot called Thor,' specifically engineered for 'transformer engines.' Concurrently, a detailed technical diagram materialized, depicting a cyclical relationship among the 'GROOT FOUNDATION MODEL,' 'ISAAC LAB IN OMNIVERSE,' and 'GROOT STACK,' interconnected with DGX, OVX, and AGX hardware, featuring a humanoid robot (GROOT) on the left and visuals of Jetson Thor hardware on the right. Jensen Huang then initiated a video, triggering a swift compilation of historical and contemporary robotics and AI imagery, encompassing a monochrome robot arm, a Stanford Research Institute schematic, a NASA lunar lander, and a matrix of current robotics showcases, all accompanied by stirring music and a female voiceover emphasizing invention and progress. The segment culminated in a 3D simulation within Isaac Sim, where a white robot arm interacted with a yellow block on a blue grid, complemented by an 'NVBLOX Example' inset demonstrating a hand gesture and its 3D rendering, all set against the ongoing musical score and voiceover.

event_blocks.summary: A real-world 'Isaac Manipulator' robotic arm, distinguished by its black and white end-effector, precisely lifted a wooden block and interacted with a mug on a black table, accompanied by mechanical whirs and clicks, as a narrator stated, "We push it to fail, so it can learn." This action transitioned to a simulated 'Isaac Sim in Omniverse Digital Twin' environment where a robotic arm sorted colored blocks on a checkered floor, with the narration continuing, "We teach it, then help it teach itself." A split-screen then showcased 'DexPilot,' juxtaposing a human hand demonstrating a task with a robot mimicking it, while the narrator explained, "We broaden its understanding to take on new challenges." In a grid-like factory simulation labeled 'Leonardo,' multiple white robotic arms performed coordinated tasks, with the narration emphasizing, "With absolute precision and succeed." The scene shifted to 'Clara Holoscan,' presenting a split-screen of a surgical robot and a digital twin of a human organ, as the narrator stated, "We make it perceive." An 'Isaac Perceptor' warehouse simulation depicted a robot navigating aisles with thermal overlays, paralleled by a real-world robot, as the narration added, "And move." An autonomous vehicle simulation, 'DRIVE Sim in Omniverse Digital Twin,' displayed sensor data and a realistic city street, followed by a first-person driving perspective with AI-generated navigation paths, with the narrator concluding, "And even reason, so it can share our world with us." Further examples included a red and black 'ElectriCSheep' tractor in a field, a 'Pegatron' factory simulation of a robotic arm performing assembly, and 'Orbita for Surgery' demonstrating a robotic arm alongside a surgeon. The segment concluded with a top-down view of a 'Covariant OFER' robotic arm sorting produce like tomatoes and limes, and a large-scale 'Amazon Robotics' warehouse simulation featuring blue robotic carts moving boxes through an automated facility, all underscored by consistent, slightly dramatic and inspiring background music and continuous mechanical sound effects.

event_blocks.summary: An upbeat, futuristic musical score accompanies the video, opening with a high-angle view of an 'Omniverse Digital Twin' simulation of an 'Amazon Robotics' warehouse, where blue robotic carts efficiently move boxes, setting the stage for "the next frontier." The scene transitions to a lab where a woman in a gray shirt interacts with a white humanoid robot, which mimics her hand wave, with a 3D simulation displayed on a large screen. This is introduced as 'NVIDIA Project GROOT,' a general-purpose foundation model for humanoid robot learning, demonstrated by multiple humanoid robots navigating an obstacle course under engineer observation. The 'Groot' model processes multimodal instructions and past interactions to produce robot actions, as illustrated by a diagram showing input from language, videos, and demonstrations. 'Isaac Lab,' a robot learning application, trains 'Groot' on 'Omniverse Isaac Sim,' with simulations showing robots training on complex terrain through thousands of iterations. A detailed data pipeline diagram outlines the flow from 'NVIDIA Isaac Sim,' text, video, and real robot data through 'NVIDIA OSMO' and heterogeneous compute to 'NVIDIA Project GROOT' and the humanoid robot, detailing 'NVIDIA DGX Train' and 'NVIDIA OVX Simulate.' 'Osmo,' a compute orchestration service, coordinates workflows across DGX and OVX systems for training and simulation, enabling 'Groot' to be trained in simulation and transferred zero-shot to the real world, as evidenced by a split-screen comparison of simulated and real kitchen environments where a teal robot performs tasks. Further demonstrations include a Fourier Intelligence robot playing drums, a humanoid robot mimicking a dancer's movements, and a 'G1' robot responding to voice commands like "Hi, G1. Can you give me a high five?" and "Can you give us some cool moves?" with distinct robot movement sounds. This intelligence is powered by new Jetson Thor Robotics chips, designed for 'GROOT,' with a final scene showing a teal and black humanoid robot walking outdoors, showcasing its mobility and environmental navigation, with 'Isaac Lab,' 'Osmo,' and 'GROOT' providing the foundational building blocks.

The video opens with a dark stage illuminated by focused lighting, showcasing a lineup of humanoid robots of varying designs and colors. The date 'MARCH 18, 2024' and the 'y/ highlights' logo are displayed. As the camera pans, NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang, dressed in a black textured leather jacket, black pants, and white sneakers, walks onto the stage, holding a small black remote. He gestures toward the robots, then moves to the right as two smaller, boxy robots with rectangular heads and glowing yellow eyes begin to walk toward him with smooth, coordinated movements, accompanied by distinct whirring and clicking mechanical sounds. Jensen Huang stands still, observing them, and introduces them, stating, "Ladies and gentlemen, this, this is Orange and this is the famous Green. They are the BDX robots of Disney. Amazing Disney Research." The robots continue to move closer, one of them turning its head to face him, demonstrating advanced mobility and interaction capabilities. Jensen Huang then urges them, "Come on, you guys, let's wrap up. Let's go," and directs them, "I said right here." As the robots emit more distinct chirping and squawking sounds, he playfully asks, "What are you saying?" and interprets their noises, "No, it's not time to eat. It's not time to eat. I'll give you a snack in a moment. Let me finish up real quick." He concludes by encouraging, "Come on, Green, hurry up," as the futuristic, high-tech atmosphere emphasizes the robots' design and functionality in the minimalist, dark environment.

event_blocks.summary: On a dark stage, NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang, clad in his signature leather jacket, playfully introduces a small, boxy robot with glowing yellow eyes, which walks unsteadily beside him. Huang announces a new industrial revolution driven by generative AI, emphasizing that every data center should be accelerated and that a trillion dollars worth of installed data centers will be modernized. He introduces the NVIDIA Blackwell Platform as the computer of this generation, designed for AI generators and capable of handling trillions of parameters. The stage is lit, with on-screen text displaying 'MARCH 18, 2024' and the 'y/ highlights' logo. Huang explains the concept of NIMs (NVIDIA Infrastructure Modules) as a new way to distribute AI software, allowing users to take their intelligence with them, and how these NIMs will enable the creation of new applications integrated like teams. He highlights NVIDIA's AI Foundry, combining NIMs, Nemo, and DGX Cloud, to help create proprietary applications and chatbots. Huang then declares that all future moving entities will be robotic, introducing Omniverse as the operating system for the robotics world, encompassing humanoid robots, autonomous vehicles, and robotic factories. He reveals the Blackwell Platform hardware, including NVLink switches and networking systems, describing the system design as a miracle. The audience erupts in applause as a sleek, dark futuristic spaceship navigates a vibrant nebula, initiating a sequence showcasing NVIDIA technologies. Inside the spaceship, the NVIDIA Onboard Assistant interface is activated, leading to a high-tech server environment where the spaceship flies past Blackwell GPUs, Grace CPUs, ConnectX-800G InfiniBand SuperNICs, NVLink SP, and NVLink Spine, culminating in a view of massive liquid-cooled server units, symbolizing peak performance and the scale of the system.

raw_segments: The video opens on a dark stage where a small, boxy robot with glowing yellow eyes, a rectangular head, and two antennae, resembling a retro-futuristic design with a white and brown body, walks unsteadily. The NVIDIA CEO, Jensen Huang, dressed in a black textured leather jacket, black shirt, black pants, and white sneakers, enters from stage left, playfully stating "Stop wasting time." He gestures toward the robot, then spreads his arms wide as if presenting it, continuing to speak and gesture while holding a small black remote. The robot continues its jerky walk in a small circle beside him. Huang announces "Five things. Five things. First, a new industrial revolution," explaining that "Every data center should be accelerated" and that "A trillion dollars worth of installed data centers will become modernized." He adds that "a new way of doing software has emerged, generative AI," which will create "new infrastructure, dedicated to doing one thing and one thing only... AI generators." As he speaks, the scene transitions to a large screen displaying a diagram titled 'A NEW INDUSTRIAL REVOLUTION' with four white line-drawn icons: 'NEW INDUSTRY' ($100 T GENERATIVE AI ACCELERATED COMPUTING), 'BLACKWELL PLATFORM', 'NIMs', and 'OMNIVERSE AND ISAAC ROBOTICS'. Huang continues, "A new industrial revolution. Second, the computer of this revolution, the computer of this generation, generative AI, trillion parameters, Blackwell. Insane amounts of computers and computing." He then humorously interjects, "Third, I'm trying to concentrate," as he returns to center stage, continuing his presentation with expressive hand gestures. The stage remains dark with focused lighting on the speaker and robot, and the on-screen text 'MARCH 18, 2024' and the 'y/ highlights' logo are visible in the corners throughout.

The video opens with NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang, dressed in a black textured leather jacket, black shirt, black pants, and white sneakers, standing on a dark, minimalist stage. A small, boxy robot with a white and brown body, glowing yellow eyes, a rectangular head with two antennae, and a jerky walking gait moves across the stage beside him. On-screen text in the top right corner reads 'MARCH 18, 2024', with a purple 'y/ highlights' logo in the bottom right. Jensen Huang speaks, stating, "Good job. Third. New computer, new computer creates new types of software. New type of software should be distributed in a new way," gesturing expressively and occasionally pointing toward the robot or the audience. He continues, explaining how this software allows users to "take it with you. Because it is your intelligence. Your intelligence should be packaged up in a way that allows you to take it with you. We call them NIMs." The camera zooms in on his animated hand movements and facial expressions as he adds, "And third, these NIMs are going to help you create a new type of application for the future... you're going to integrate them like teams. Create these applications." He then gestures toward a large screen behind him, which displays a diagram titled 'A NEW INDUSTRIAL REVOLUTION' with components including 'NIMs', 'NEMO AND NVIDIA AI FOUNDRY', and 'OMNIVERSE AND ISAAC ROBOTICS'. He elaborates, "We have a fantastic capability between NIMs, the AI technology, the tools, Nemo, and the infrastructure DGX Cloud, in our AI Foundry to help you create proprietary applications, proprietary chatbots." The robot continues to move beside him, occasionally turning its head. The camera zooms out to a wide shot, showing Jensen Huang and the robot on stage with the full diagram visible behind them. He concludes, "And then lastly, everything that moves in the future will be robotic. You're not going to be the only one. And these robotic systems, whether they..." as the robot briefly turns and walks toward the center of the stage before stopping. The segment ends with Jensen Huang continuing to speak, gesturing toward the screen and the robot, emphasizing the integration of AI, robotics, and computing platforms in a new industrial era.

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang, dressed in a black leather jacket, stands on a dark, minimalist stage, gesturing expressively as he speaks. A small, boxy robot with glowing yellow eyes and a jerky gait moves beside him. On-screen text in the top right corner reads 'MARCH 18, 2024', with a purple 'y/ highlights' logo in the bottom right. Huang's clear voice explains the need for a digital platform for 'humanoid, AMRs, self-driving cars, forklifts, manipulating arms,' and 'robotic factories,' identifying it as 'Omniverse, the operating system of the robotics world.' The camera zooms in on Huang, then transitions to a wide shot revealing a large screen behind him displaying a technical diagram titled 'A NEW INDUSTRIAL REVOLUTION,' detailing components like 'BLACKWELL PLATFORM' and 'OMNIVERSE AND ISAAC ROBOTICS.' Huang refers to 'the five things that we talked about today' and then asks, 'What does NVIDIA look like?' The scene cuts to a detailed graphic of the 'NVIDIA Blackwell Platform' hardware components. Huang emphatically declares, 'This is what we announced to you today. This is Blackwell. This is the platform.' The audience, seated in a dimly lit auditorium bathed in purple lighting, is shown attentively watching, and erupts in enthusiastic applause following Huang's announcement. The scene returns to Huang on stage with the robot, gesturing toward the screen as the applause continues.

The video opens with NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang, dressed in a black textured leather jacket, black shirt, black pants, and white sneakers, holding a small black remote on a dark, minimalist stage. He speaks with enthusiasm, detailing "amazing, amazing processors, NVLink switches, networking systems," and declares, "the system design is a miracle. This is Blackwell. This to me is what a GPU looks like in my mind." The audience responds with loud, sustained applause. Jensen then asks, "I think we have one more treat for everybody. What do you think, should we?" As he speaks, a small, boxy robot with a white and brown body, glowing yellow eyes, and antennae moves across the stage with a jerky gait, emitting distinct mechanical whirring and clanking sounds. Jensen exclaims, "Okay. We have one more thing to show you. Roll it," as the robot continues its mechanical movements. The screen behind him briefly displayed 'Spectrum X800 Switch', 'BlueField-3 SuperNIC', and 'ConnectX-8 SuperNIC'. The scene transitions to a large, detailed graphic of the 'NVIDIA Blackwell Platform', showcasing various hardware components like the HGX B100 and GB200 Superchip Compute Node, with Jensen small in the foreground emphasizing its scale. A dramatic, futuristic orchestral score begins, accompanied by sci-fi sound effects, as the scene cuts to an animated sequence. A sleek, dark spaceship navigates a vibrant purple and blue nebula. Inside, a hand types 'Show me NVIDIA technologies' into a futuristic interface, which responds with 'NVIDIA Onboard Assistant' and prompts to 'initiate NeVA auto pilot', accompanied by interface beeps and typing sounds. The spaceship then accelerates, its engines glowing orange with a powerful whoosh, flying through the cosmic environment. Throughout, the date 'MARCH 18, 2024' and the 'y/ highlights' logo are visible.

The segment opens with a sleek, dark futuristic spaceship, its orange engines glowing, navigating a vibrant blue nebula amidst intense, building electronic sounds and mechanical whirring. A distinct thump accompanies its rapid movement before the scene transitions into a high-tech server environment. Here, a rhythmic, percussive electronic hum begins, as the spaceship flies through rows of server racks, symbolizing a journey through digital infrastructure. As it moves deeper, the camera reveals intricate details of server components, including labeled Blackwell GPUs and Grace CPUs, highlighted with glowing outlines, all set against a backdrop of dynamic, sweeping electronic sounds and subtle clicks, suggesting data flow and processing. The spaceship continues its flight, passing through a complex network of circuit boards and hardware, with on-screen text identifying key technologies like ConnectX-800G InfiniBand SuperNIC, NVLink SP, and NVLink Spine. The electronic soundscape intensifies, building with powering-up sounds and a rising pitch, culminating in a powerful electronic surge and a sharp ping. The journey concludes in a large data center, where the spaceship flies past massive liquid-cooled server units, emphasizing the scale and power of the system, accompanied by expansive, powerful electronic hums, whooshes, and rhythmic pulses, suggesting peak performance. Throughout this high-speed traversal, the date 'MARCH 18, 2024' and the 'y/ highlights' logo remain visible in the corners, reinforcing the context of a technology showcase.

event_blocks.summary: Intense futuristic electronic music accompanied a sleek, dark spaceship with glowing orange engines navigating a blue, bubble-filled environment, transitioning to a high-tech server room filled with 'DGX SUPERPOD' racks. The spaceship, piloted by a character in a white helmet, maneuvered through the infrastructure as the music built tension. It shifted to a triumphant orchestral piece as the scene moved to a large, dark auditorium where a massive screen displayed a cosmic nebula with the text 'Powering the New Era of Computing' and the NVIDIA logo. The audience, bathed in purple lighting, watched attentively as the NVIDIA CEO, Jensen Huang, dressed in a black leather jacket and black shirt, walked onto the stage, waving to the crowd amidst swelling cheers and applause. He stood beside a display of three open server modules labeled 'H100' and 'H200', gesturing as he began his presentation, his clear and enthusiastic voice heard saying "Thank you!", "Thank you, have a great, have a great GTC!", and "Thank you all for coming!". The minimalist and dark stage, with focused lighting on the speaker and hardware, emphasized the advanced technology, while on-screen text consistently displayed 'MARCH 18, 2024' and a 'y/ highlights' logo. The auditorium was large and dimly lit with purple ambient lighting, the stage minimalist with a massive black screen showing the NVIDIA logo flanked by monitors showing Jensen Huang. Huang, in a black textured leather jacket, black shirt, black pants, and white sneakers, walked onto the stage from the left, holding a small black remote, prompting loud, enthusiastic applause and cheering from the audience, many of whom stood to acknowledge him, with some raising hands or recording. Stage lighting intensified, focusing on Huang as he approached the center, creating a high-tech, professional, and celebratory atmosphere.
